name: Integris
description_short: INTEGRIS Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union serving INTEGRIS Health employees and their families in Oklahoma City with nearly 60 years of service.
description_long:
INTEGRIS Federal Credit Union has operated for almost 60 years as a federally-insured credit union based in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma. The institution is specifically designed to serve INTEGRIS Health employees and their immediate family members, operating under a membership model rather than as a traditional commercial bank. The credit union maintains two physical branch locations—a North Office at 2525 Northwest Expressway and a South Office—with extended business hours and 24/7 voice response services.

The organization offers a comprehensive range of financial services including VISA credit cards, home banking platforms, personal loans, and various insurance products through TruStage. Members can apply for loans online and access competitive interest rates designed for member investment. The credit union emphasizes user-friendly loan application processes and promotes its commitment to "investing in its members" through competitive lending terms. Insurance offerings include disability coverage, homeowner's insurance, life insurance, and auto insurance options.

Integris distinguishes itself through its membership-focused model and field of membership restriction to INTEGRIS Health employees and families, which creates a specialized community-oriented approach to banking. The institution highlights its commitment to convenience through digital banking services that allow members to conduct virtually all business without visiting a branch office. The emphasis on member relationships and locally-based service sets it apart from larger national institutions.

The credit union is regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA), providing federal deposit insurance up to $250,000. However, as a membership-restricted credit union, it is not accessible to the general public—potential members must have employment or family connections to INTEGRIS Health. The organization's marketing heavily emphasizes seasonal lending promotions and emergency services but provides limited public information about specific interest rates, fees, or detailed product terms on its website. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Home banking and online account management
Branch services at two Oklahoma City locations
pros: Federal NCUA insurance covers deposits up to $250,000 backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Nearly 60 years of operational history and stability serving the same member community
Online loan application and approval process available without visiting a branch
Comprehensive insurance products through TruStage covering disability, homeowner, life, and auto coverage
Competitive interest rates emphasized as core philosophy with commitment to member investment
24/7 voice response service available at (405) 948-7272 for member assistance
Digital home banking platform allowing members to conduct most financial business remotely
cons: Membership restricted to INTEGRIS Health employees and their families only—not accessible to general public
Limited publicly available information on specific interest rates, APRs, and fee schedules on website
Only two branch locations in Oklahoma City area, limiting accessibility for distributed membership
Website lacks detailed product comparison information or transparent pricing for loans and credit cards
No apparent mobile app information or modern digital banking features prominently advertised
best_for: INTEGRIS Health employees seeking banking services from a trusted member-focused institution
Families of INTEGRIS Health employees wanting to consolidate finances with employer-connected credit union
Members prioritizing personalized service and community-oriented lending approach over national bank convenience
Borrowers seeking competitive rates and user-friendly loan applications from a federally-insured institution
diagnosis: INTEGRIS Federal Credit Union is best for current and former INTEGRIS Health employees and their family members who want community-focused banking with federal insurance protection and competitive lending rates. The primary caveat is that membership is strictly limited to the INTEGRIS Health employee community, making it inaccessible to the general public regardless of creditworthiness or financial needs.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
